data.title: Irish MMA Star Conor McGregor Calls for Country to Build Strategic Bitcoin Reserves
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Irish mixed martial arts star Conor McGregor (Conor McGregor) posted on the X platform calling on the country to establish a strategic Bitcoin reserve. He said: "The origin of cryptocurrency is to return power to the people. The Irish Bitcoin Strategic Reserve will empower the people's currency. I will co-host a Twitter Space meeting to discuss the changes I hope to see."

data.title: Analysts have mixed opinions on Coinbase's acquisition of Deribit: Derivatives expansion is optimistic, but retail growth is questionable
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Coinbase chose to reveal the news of its acquisition of Deribit to the market before the U.S. stock market closed and released its financial report on Thursday. Benchmark analyst Mark Palmer pointed out that as more institutions adopt cryptocurrencies, the acquisition of Deribit will allow Coinbase to "immediately dominate the fast-growing derivatives field." Oppenheimer analyst Owen Lau believes that this move will make Coinbase a "strong challenger" to Binance, Bybit and OKX in the derivatives field, and emphasizes that "cryptocurrency options have weak cyclicality and have stable demand regardless of whether the market is rising or falling." However, Compass Point analysts Ed Engel and Joe Flynn pointed out that Deribit mainly serves institutional clients and does not help to increase Coinbase's retail-based perpetual contract trading volume. The two analysts downgraded Coinbase's rating to sell last week and reiterated that its U.S. retail trading market has become saturated. Michael Klena of Architect Partners believes that this acquisition will not fundamentally change its assessment of Coinbase (target price $170), after all, it is just an extension of the existing business and cannot reduce the impact of cryptocurrency market fluctuations on quarterly performance.

data.title: Cointelegraph's Twitter Account Allegedly Compromised, Investors Warned of Risks
data.description: According to BlockBeats, community reports suggest that Cointelegraph's official Twitter account may have been compromised. The account reportedly shared a contract for the Meme coin 'TELE,' which has since been rug-pulled. Investors are advised to exercise caution regarding associated risks.

data.title: ZachXBT: Law enforcement mistakenly detained former Yuga Labs security researcher in a 2022 cyber theft case
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Chain detective ZachXBT said that law enforcement mistakenly detained former Yuga Labs security researcher Sam Curry for a 2022 cyber theft that stole about $1.1 million worth of BAYC NFTs. ZachXBT said he had identified the wallet behind the scam and associated it with a now-deleted X account.
In December 2022, an anonymous attacker stole 14 Bored Ape Yacht Club NFTs, each of which was trading for about $86,000 at the time. Law enforcement then "mistakenly viewed OpenSea's logs, which contained [Curry's] home IP address, and used this address to mistakenly list him as a suspect." (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Indonesian government may ask Worldcoin to delete 500,000 retinal data
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Indonesia's Ministry of Communications and Digital (Komdigi) may delete 500,000 retinal data records collected by the Worldcoin platform, a move stemming from concerns about potential data leaks. Komdigi is investigating whether there are risks in storing retinal data. If evidence of such risks is found, the data may be permanently deleted.
Worldcoin, a platform that provides identity verification, has come under scrutiny for offering 800,000 rupiah ($48.34) to users who scan their retinas. After Komdigi revoked its operating license, the platform stopped all scanning activities in Indonesia. Worldcoin clarified that its cryptocurrency Worldcoin is not used for compensation and that these tokens are optional incentives.

data.title: A whale withdrew more than 9,000 ETH from Bybit in the past two days
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens monitoring, in the past two days, a whale deposited 20.18 million USDT to Bybit and withdrew 9023.5 ETH at a price of 2237 US dollars. Currently, the whale address holds 11739 ETH and 9.38 million USDT worth 27.57 million US dollars.

data.title: Cointelegraph's X Account Potentially Compromised
data.description: According to Odaily, Cointelegraph's X account appears to have been hacked. The account has been posting information about on-chain tokens and has disabled comments. Users are advised to remain vigilant.

data.title: Indonesia May Delete Retinal Data Amid Security Concerns
data.description:
According to PANews, the Indonesian Ministry of Communication and Digital (Komdigi) is considering the deletion of 500,000 retinal data records collected by the World platform due to concerns over potential data breaches. Komdigi is currently investigating the risks associated with storing retinal data. If evidence of such risks is found, the data may be permanently deleted.

World, a platform providing identity verification services, has faced scrutiny for offering users 800,000 Indonesian Rupiah (approximately $48.34) to scan their retinas. Following the revocation of its operating license by Komdigi, World has ceased all scanning activities in Indonesia. The platform clarified that its cryptocurrency, Worldcoin, is not used as compensation; instead, these tokens are optional incentives.

data.title: BlackRock meets with SEC cryptocurrency panel to discuss collateral and options on crypto ETFs
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the minutes of the relevant meeting, representatives of BlackRock, the world's largest asset management company, met with staff from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Friday. The theme was how to solve problems related to the regulation of crypto assets. The meeting discussed the pledge and options of crypto ETFs.
It is reported that Robert Mitchnick, head of digital assets at BlackRock, also attended the meeting. BlackRock representatives discussed "relevant considerations for ETP products that support pledge functions" with staff from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), and discussed parameters such as position calculations and exercise limits for cryptocurrency ETF options, including liquidity thresholds. In addition, BlackRock also discussed with the SEC the general standards for approving crypto ETFs.

data.title: Cointelegraph's official account is suspected to have been stolen, please remain vigilant
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Cointelegraph's X account is suspected to have been hacked. It published on-chain token information and closed comments. Users are advised to remain vigilant.

data.title: BlackRock and SEC Discuss Crypto Asset Regulation Strategies
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, representatives from BlackRock, the world's largest asset management firm, met with staff from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) this Friday to address methods for regulating crypto assets. The meeting focused on issues related to crypto ETFs, including staking and options.

Robert Mitchnick, BlackRock's Head of Digital Assets, was present at the meeting. Discussions between BlackRock representatives and SEC staff covered considerations for ETP products that support staking functions. They also deliberated on parameters for crypto ETF options, such as position calculations, exercise limits, and liquidity thresholds. Additionally, BlackRock and the SEC explored general standards for approving crypto ETFs.

data.title: Bitcoin mining company TeraWulf reports net loss of $61.4 million in the first quarter
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bitcoin mining company TeraWulf announced its first quarter financial report for 2025, with a net loss of $61.4 million, much higher than the $9.6 million in the same period last year. The company's revenue fell year-on-year to $34.4 million, while revenue costs rose to $24.5 million, accounting for 71.4%, more than double the 34% in the same period last year. TeraWulf attributed the decline in revenue to the halving of Bitcoin and the increase in network difficulty. In addition, severe weather in New York State also affected its mine operations.

data.title: US Vice President JD Vance to Speak at Bitcoin 2025
data.description:
US VP JD Vance to speak at Bitcoin 2025, focusing on financial independence, innovation in Las Vegas.

data.title: TeraWulf Reports Significant Losses in Q1 2025 Financial Results
data.description: According to Odaily, Bitcoin mining company TeraWulf has released its financial results for the first quarter of 2025, revealing a net loss of $61.4 million. This figure is considerably higher than the $9.6 million loss reported in the same period last year. The company's revenue decreased to $34.4 million, while the cost of revenue rose to $24.5 million, accounting for 71.4% of total revenue, more than double the 34% from the previous year. TeraWulf attributes the decline in revenue to the Bitcoin halving and increased network difficulty. Additionally, adverse weather conditions in New York have impacted the operation of its mining facilities.

source: https://www.cointrackdaily.com/coinbase-steers-clear-of-bitcoin-bet-balancing-ambition-with-prudence/

data.title: Coinbase CEO: I considered adopting Michael Saylor’s Bitcoin investment strategy, but ultimately did not adopt it because the risk was too high
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Coinbase had considered adopting the Bitcoin buying strategy popularized by Michael Saylor in the past, but believed that the plan was too risky. "We have indeed had such a moment in the past 12 years, whether we should put 80% of our assets into cryptocurrencies-specifically Bitcoin," said Brian Armstrong, CEO of Coinbase, in a video speech on the X social network. Coinbase ultimately decided to abandon this strategy because it could damage the company's cash position as a startup and could even "kill" the San Francisco-based company. Despite this, according to the company's latest shareholder letter, Coinbase purchased $153 million worth of cryptocurrencies (mainly Bitcoin) in the first quarter. According to the letter, the company holds $1.3 billion worth of cryptocurrencies (mainly Bitcoin) as investments. (Bloomberg)

data.title: A PEPE whale transferred 500 billion PEPE to Binance 1 hour ago
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain data analyst Yu Jin, the whale address starting with 0x3c9 transferred 500 billion PEPE (worth about 6.14 million US dollars) to Binance 1 hour ago. 
Currently, there are still 1.5 trillion PEPE in this address, worth about 12.32 million US dollars.

data.title: Fidelity Sees Significant Inflow in Bitcoin Fund
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Farside Investors reported a net inflow of $45 million into Fidelity's Bitcoin fund (FBTC) yesterday. Meanwhile, the net inflow/outflow for Fidelity's Ethereum fund (FETH) remained at zero.
